title: The Creators of Claude Code and OpenClaw don't Prompt Their Agents Anymore?!
summary: AI engineers use loops that automatically prompt agents rather than manual prompting. The technique involves an orchestrator agent spawning worker agents in parallel to complete tasks, with observability and cost control via dashboards. This compounds both capability and hallucination risk.
writeup: Cole Medin examines a workflow where AI engineers stop prompting agents by hand and instead run loops that prompt the agents automatically. The core pattern is an orchestrator agent that spawns worker agents in parallel to complete tasks, with dashboards providing observability and cost control. The tradeoff is direct: the approach compounds capability, and it compounds hallucination risk along with it.
